topic.name: Marine Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and acts aimed at protecting marine ecosystems and species through emergency response, regulation of marine transportation, and management of marine mammal resources.

Document 1: Authorizes the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to declare and respond to a marine disease emergency
Establishes the Marine Disease Emergency Fund for responding to those emergencies.
Document 2: Exempts from certain provisions limiting the transportation of passengers by a passenger vessel between U.S. coastwise trading ports
Excludes from the existing definition of 'passenger vessel': public vessels, vessels owned or operated by a state or local government.
Document 3: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the transport, purchase, or sale of any pelt of a Southcentral or Southeast Alaskan otter
any handicraft, garment, or art produced from such a pelt.

topic.name: Public Assistance Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at providing support and resources for child care, fire safety, and elder care through grants and tax credits.

Document 1: establish a business-incentive grant program to provide child care through public-private partnerships
cover up to one-third of the costs for employee child care services
Document 2: authorize the Administrator to award assistance to firefighters grants
increase the caps on these grants, particularly for departments that serve larger communities
Document 3: allow caregivers a tax credit for up to $6,000 of the eldercare expenses
require the Department of Health and Human Services to award a grant to establish a National Resource Center on Family Caregiving

topic.name: Oil and Gas Exploration Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and moratoriums related to oil and gas exploration and extraction activities in various U.S. coastal and offshore areas.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coast of New Jersey.
a moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 2: This bill prohibits conducting geological or geophysical activities in support of oil or gas exploration and development in any area located within a specified exclusive economic zone located off the coastline of Florida.

This moratorium shall only be terminated if the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration...determines that the reasonably foreseeable impacts of such activities are minimal to individuals or populations of marine mammals, sea turtles, or fish.
Document 3: the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources to the United States
it is national policy to actively advance oil and gas development within the NPR.

topic.name: Legislative Designations and Conveyances
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions related to the designation of facilities and the conveyance of federal properties to local entities.

Document 1: This bill requires the Department of the Interior to convey to the Arbuckle Master Conservancy District...
the Complex and Office shall not be considered to be a part of a federal reclamation project.
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2 Government Center in Fall River, Massachusetts, as the 'Sergeant Robert Barrett Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 427 Harrison Avenue in Harrison, New Jersey, as the 'Patricia D. McGinty-Juhl Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Legal Reforms on Terrorism and Justice
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at addressing issues related to terrorism, the judicial process, and the rights of individuals within legal proceedings.

Document 1: expand the prohibition against conveying false information and hoaxes to include false information and hoaxes about any federal crime of terrorism
increase maximum prison terms for hoaxes involving a member of the Armed Forces during war
Document 2: authorize the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings
inform each witness who is not a party of the right to make such request
Document 3: require a federal or state court to substitute the United States for an electronic communication service provider
designed to detect or prevent a terrorist attack against the United States

topic.name: Healthcare Legislation and Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to healthcare services and payment methodologies under Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
require payment under the Medicaid program for services furnished by recipients of Ryan White Part C grants
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
repeal the caps on Medicare outpatient rehabilitation physical therapy services
Document 3: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
specify that, with respect to the use of private contracts between Medicare beneficiaries and physicians, a 'physician' includes a licensed chiropractor

topic.name: Social Security and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to adjustments and eligibility for social security, veterans' pensions, and employee leave benefits.

Document 1: increase of $150 in Social Security benefits
require an annual adjustment in the OASDI contribution and benefit base
Document 2: amends the Family and Medical Leave Act
employee meets the hours of service eligibility requirement
Document 3: exempts payments from annual income determinations
payments for pain and suffering related to accidents or losses

topic.name: Veterans Education Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving educational assistance and support for veterans and service members pursuing higher education.

Document 1: Amends the Montgomery GI Bill educational assistance program for veterans
provide a stipend for individuals using such assistance to pursue a program of education
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to prohibit members of the Armed Forces Reserves from being deemed ineligible for participation in federal TRIO programs due to age
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend by one year the Secretary of Education's authority to purchase Federal Family Education Loans

topic.name: Aviation Infrastructure Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the financing and development of aviation infrastructure, including airport improvements and funding mechanisms.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2010 for Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) air traffic control services
Revises requirements authorizing the imposition of passenger facility charges.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations through the three-month period beginning on October 1, 2009, for airport improvement program (AIP) projects
Extends through FY2010 the termination date of any order issued by the Secretary with respect to ...
Document 3: Allows a passenger facility fee imposed at a large hub airport to be used as an eligible project cost to carry out noise mitigation for certain school buildings
if the buildings are adversely affected by airport noise.

topic.name: Child Abuse Reporting Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the reporting and prevention of child abuse and sexual misconduct in educational and residential settings.

Document 1: requires disciplining of any school employee who fails to report sexual misconduct
establishes policies for investigating and reporting such allegations
Document 2: prohibits federal funding to organizations that fail to report child sexual abuse
requires individuals in contact with children to report known and suspected instances of child abuse
Document 3: requires minimum standards for programs serving children
establishes a national toll-free hotline to receive complaints

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at promoting and regulating various forms of renewable energy production and technology advancements in the United States.

Document 1: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the cellulosic biofuel producer tax credit
expand the definition of 'qualified feedstock' to include any cultivated algae, cyanobacteria, or lemna
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a direct loan program for high risk geothermal exploration wells
Authorizes the Secretary to base the cost share percentage for loans on a sliding scale
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to authorize the President to waive the requirement concerning the applicable volume of renewable fuels
if the President finds that complying with such requirement in that year is not technologically feasible

topic.name: Employee Rights and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at safeguarding employee rights, particularly in the context of layoffs, furloughs, and flexible working conditions.

Document 1: Requires an air carrier that furloughs an airline employee to retain such employee on a recall list for not less than 10 years after the furlough.
Authorizes a furloughed employee... to bring an action in U.S. district court to enforce this Act.
Document 2: Requires an employer to give 90-day written notice... before ordering a plant closing or mass layoff.
Authorizes an affected employee to file a complaint with the Secretary alleging a violation of the notice requirements.
Document 3: Authorizes an employee to request from an employer a temporary or permanent change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment.
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.

topic.name: Veterans Health and Support Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing health care and support services for veterans and active members of the Armed Forces, particularly in relation to mental health and disability evaluations.

Document 1: transfer of information and sharing of disability examination procedures
physical examination shall be performed by a VA-certified health care provider
Document 2: expand the definition of 'health care provider'
incentive payments for the meaningful use of certified EHR technology
Document 3: eligible for readjustment counseling and related mental health services
enhance awareness of post-traumatic-stress-disorder (PTSD)

topic.name: Nuclear Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation, licensing, and safety of nuclear power facilities and materials in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to require...
Conditions renewal of any such license on the same criteria and requirements applicable to an original application.
Document 2: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) from issuing a license...
Authorizes the President to waive such prohibition and authorize the grant of a specific license to import materials...
Document 3: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) from approving the request of a licensee for a waiver...
Specifies as a covered regulation: (1) an emergency preparedness or response regulation...

topic.name: Legislative Changes in Criminal Justice and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses recent legislative amendments aimed at improving criminal justice processes and healthcare treatment related to addiction and mental health.

Document 1: Opiate Addiction Treatment Act of 2011 - Amends the Controlled Substances Act to authorize a nurse practitioner with a graduate nursing degree to dispense certain narcotic drugs
for maintenance treatment or detoxification treatment
Document 2: Fix Gun Checks Act of 2011 - Amends the NICS Improvement Amendments Act of 2007
to increase the amount of a state's allocation under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program
Document 3: Enhanced Financial Recovery and Equitable Retirement Treatment Act of 2007 - Imposes surcharges for unpaid criminal monetary penalties
Establishes in the Treasury the Department of Justice Enhanced Financial Recovery Fund to support Department of Justice debt collection activities

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer protection in financial transactions and privacy.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Secretary of the Treasury to make certain disclosures to the holder of a social security account number
Requires employers who have been notified of suspected misuse of an employee's social security account number to cease including such account number on statements.
Document 2: Sets forth a limited antitrust immunity to providers of covered electronic payment systems and merchants for negotiation of access rates and terms
Requires issuers, acquirers, owners, and merchants to make specified disclosures regarding itemized costs and access agreements.
Document 3: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement any financial institution
Does not share information with affiliates under the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code impacting retirement accounts and small business savings.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to suspend in 2008, 2009, and 2010 requirements for minimum distributions from tax-deferred retirement plans.
Permits taxpayers who have already received a minimum distribution in 2008 or before July 1, 2009, to recontribute such distribution.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for tax-exempt Small Business Start-up Savings Accounts.
Sets forth rules for the tax treatment of contributions to and rollovers from such accounts.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts and research initiatives aimed at regulating environmental impacts and managing natural resources, particularly in relation to water pollution and fisheries.

Document 1: amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
require a permit under the national pollutant discharge elimination system for discharges of stormwater runoff
Document 2: adds the Tennessee counties of Hickman, Perry, and Wayne to the definition of 'Appalachian region'
Document 3: authorizes the U.S. Geological Survey to conduct critical monitoring, scientific assessments, and research in support of fisheries
execute a comprehensive, multi-lake, freshwater fisheries science program

topic.name: U.S. Financial Sanctions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the imposition of financial sanctions against foreign entities and individuals involved in activities that threaten U.S. national security, particularly concerning terrorism and specific countries like Iran and Cuba.

Document 1: prohibit, or impose strict conditions on, the opening or maintaining in the United States of a correspondent account

impose specified sanctions against any person who knowingly engages in a transaction with a foreign person that transfers or receives, or facilitates the transfer or receipt of, any goods or services to or from ISIS.
Document 2: prohibits the President from issuing a license that permits a person to conduct an offshore U.S. dollar clearing system for transactions involving the government of Iran
The President shall block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of any listed institution if the property and interests: (1) are in the United States.
Document 3: amends the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000 to exempt from prohibitions against U.S. assistance to Cuba any exports under the market access program

A person subject to U.S. jurisdiction may invest in the development of an agricultural business in Cuba if the Department of State and the Department of Agriculture jointly determine that the agricultural business: is not controlled by the government of Cuba.

topic.name: Federal Funding Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal funding, appropriations, and program terminations across various governmental departments and initiatives.

Document 1: Prohibits federal expenditures for specified programs in various departments
Deems any authorization in law for such programs to be repealed.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the National Science Foundation (NSF) for FY2008-FY2010
Sets minimum and maximum amounts of awards under the Major Research Instrumentation program.
Document 3: Requires the National Science Foundation to establish a Global Warming Education Program
A competitive grant program to provide grants to states, municipalities, educational institutions, and other organizations.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts for Social Causes
topic.description: This cluster includes various legislative acts aimed at addressing social issues such as honoring veterans, combating child trafficking, and promoting environmental sustainability in Haiti.

Document 1: Directs the President to issue an annual proclamation calling on the people of the United States to observe two minutes of silence on Veterans Day
in honor of the service and sacrifice of veterans throughout the history of the nation.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of State, through the Ambassador-at-Large of the Department of State's Office to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons, to provide assistance for an eligible country
to support policies and programs to eradicate the trafficking of children.
Document 3: Authorizes the President to provide the government of Haiti with financial assistance, technology transfers, or capacity building assistance to reduce deforestation
to promote local participation in the design, implementation, and independent monitoring of policies and initiatives.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at reforming and enhancing various aspects of agricultural policy, including payments, conservation, and public access to land.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
permit a producer to reduce a covered commodity base acreage
Document 2: Amends the Food Security Act of 1985
encourage owners and operators of privately-held farm, ranch, and forest land to voluntarily make such land available for public access

Document 3: Revises agricultural and related programs
producer income; conservation; trade; nutrition

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Land Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses the processes and legislative measures related to the federal recognition of Native American tribes and their land rights, including the management and benefits associated with such recognition.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take land into trust for the Tribe.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a loan program to assist the Crow Tribe
Allows the Secretary to accept from any eligible individual a donation of land or an interest in land within the Reservation.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to recognize Alexander Creek, Alaska, as a Native village
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to negotiate an agreement with Alexander Creek to settle aboriginal land claims.

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on U.S. national security interests through international reporting and cooperation on various geopolitical issues.

Document 1: Amends the United States-Hong Kong Policy Act of 1992 to direct the Secretary of State to report to Congress on conditions in Hong Kong that are of U.S. interest
certify to Congress annually whether Hong Kong is sufficiently autonomous to justify separate treatment different from that accorded to China
Document 2: directs the Department of Defense to report to Congress annually on cooperation between Iran and Russia and how such cooperation affects U.S. national security
the likelihood of Iran adopting the Russian model of hybrid warfare against potential targets such as the Gulf Cooperation Council states
Document 3: Directs the President to report annually to Congress regarding foreign countries' use of information and communications technologies in critical infrastructure
Urges the President to take restrictive actions against a country that has not complied with the appropriate benchmarks with respect to foreign assistance

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing accountability and transparency in federal agency rulemaking processes.

Document 1: Amends the Congressional Review Act to require congressional approval of major rules issued by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA).
Expands the criteria for an EPA regulation to be deemed a major rule to include that the rule will have a significant impact on a substantial number of agricultural entities.
Document 2: Revises rulemaking requirements under the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995 to require federal agencies to prepare and publish an initial and final regulatory impact analysis.
Extends UMRA requirements to independent regulatory agencies.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to publish notice in the Federal Register of the Secretary's intent to prepare such a document at least three months before issuance of a draft.
Requires the Secretary to conduct a retrospective analysis of a final Level 1 guidance document not less than every five years.

topic.name: U.S. Tax and Postal Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to taxation and postal service regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the U.S. Postal Service to designate a single ZIP Code applicable to the area encompassing only the city of Windsor Heights, Iowa.
Document 2: Grants the consent of Congress to the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement.
Authorizes each state that is a party to the Agreement to require all remote sellers to collect and remit sales and use taxes.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from requiring a payor of interest to file an information return on interest that is not effectively connected with a trade or business within the United States.

topic.name: Federal Budget Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts that influence federal budgeting, appropriations, and accounting practices.

Document 1: Requires the receipts and disbursements of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to be counted as new budget authority.
Subjects to the statutory public debt limit the face amount of obligations issued by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Document 2: States that the budgetary effects of this Act for the purpose of complying with the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 shall be determined by reference to the latest statement titled 'Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation'.
Document 3: Makes appropriations available each fiscal year for operations of the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy.
Requires funds made available for a fiscal year under this Act to remain available until the enactment of an appropriation for such fiscal year.

topic.name: Health Insurance Accessibility
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to health insurance for Americans through various mechanisms and reforms.

Document 1: Directs the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to administer a health insurance program for non-federal employees
Requires OPM to ensure that covered individuals are in a risk pool separate from that maintained for federal employees.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow uninsured individual taxpayers and their spouses and dependents a refundable tax credit for a limited amount of their health insurance costs

Amends the Social Security Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to deem whether a state has taken efforts to provide its citizens with greater access to affordable private health insurance.
Document 3: Amends the McCarran-Ferguson Act to provide that nothing in that Act shall modify, impair, or supersede the operation of any of the antitrust laws with respect to the business of health insurance.
Applies prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance.

topic.name: Musculoskeletal and Health Services
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives focused on improving musculoskeletal health and related services in the United States.

Document 1: Access to America's Orthopaedic Services Act of 2009
reporting requirements related to musculoskeletal health
Document 2: amends the Public Health Service Act
services for prevention and treatment of diabetes through the Indian Health Service

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Accountability and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability in government operations and assessing national security implications related to various issues, including climate change and healthcare spending.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a National Intelligence Estimate on the anticipated geopolitical effects of global climate change and the implications of such effects on U.S. national security.
Requires the Secretary of State to report to Congress on the potential for large migration flows in countries of strategic interest or humanitarian concern as a response to changes in climate.
Document 2: Repeals sections of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care (PPACA) related to the establishment of an Independent Payment Advisory Board (IPAD).
Document 3: Requires the head of each federal agency to submit to the Recovery Accountability and Transparency Board (RATB) all information concerning the agency's programs, priority goals, and results for publication online.

topic.name: Counterterrorism Measures
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative actions and restrictions related to terrorism and national security.

Document 1: impose specified travel and other related restrictions on heads of state of countries that are state sponsors of terrorism
authorize the President to waive such restrictions if in the national interest
Document 2: grounds for loss of U.S. nationality by a native-born or naturalized citizen
providing training or material assistance to any foreign terrorist organization
Document 3: prohibits any funds made available to the Department of Defense from being obligated or expended to increase the number of U.S. Armed Forces serving in Iraq

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety standards and management practices in various transportation sectors, including highways, motorcoaches, and natural gas storage facilities.

Document 1: Requires a state to develop and implement a highway bridge management system that meets certain requirements.
Identify corrosion mitigation and prevention methods to preserve its highway bridges.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations to require monitoring of the safety performance of motorcoach service providers.
Establish minimum training requirements for motorcoach drivers.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to issue minimum uniform safety standards for the operation, environmental protection, and integrity management of underground natural gas storage facilities.
Consider the economic impacts of the regulations on individual gas customers.

topic.name: Water Resource Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the planning, construction, and management of water reclamation and supply projects in the southwestern United States.

Document 1: expands the current authorization for the construction or rehabilitation and operation and maintenance of conjunctive use wells
increasing from 2% to 4% the portion of funds that may be used for the survey, recovery, protection, preservation, and display of archaeological resources
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with local authorities, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities needed to reclaim, reuse, and treat groundwater and wastewater
Limits the federal share of the project to 25% of the total cost
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to complete a feasibility study of alternatives to augment water supplies
Limits the federal share of total costs of the study to 45%

topic.name: Carbon Emission Reduction Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reducing carbon emissions through various programs and regulatory measures.

Document 1: Authorizes qualified industry organizations to conduct a referendum among the owners or operators of distribution utilities delivering fossil fuel-based electricity for the creation of a Carbon Storage Research Corporation.
Requires the Corporation to support large-scale demonstrations of carbon capture and storage (CCS) technologies capable of advancing the technologies to commercial readiness.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a program to govern the creation of credits from emission reductions from uncapped domestic sources and sinks.
Establishes the Carbon Conservation Fund for the carbon conservation program.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to direct the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate regulations necessary to reduce the aggregate net level of global warming pollution emissions.
Establishes a market-based emissions cap and global warming pollutants trading program.

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and policies aimed at protecting the rights of individuals based on sexual orientation and gender identity.

Document 1: military chaplain to lead a prayer according to the dictates of the chaplain's conscience.
Document 2: prohibits personnel actions from being made on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 3: prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating in... on the basis of their actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.

topic.name: Immigration and Employment Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating employment practices for nonimmigrant workers in the United States, including visa requirements and protections for U.S. workers.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise employer and government requirements with respect to H-1B and L-1 nonimmigrant aliens.
Prohibits blanket L-1 petitions.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Labor to take specified actions to recruit U.S. workers before petitioning to hire H-2B workers.
Prohibits the Secretary from granting a temporary labor certification until U.S. nationals are not qualified or available.

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing benefits and support for veterans and their families.

Document 1: increase by up to $1,000 a month the rate of the Medal of Honor special pension
Document 2: payment of dependency and indemnity compensation to the survivors of veterans rated totally disabled
survivors of such veterans who died after September 30, 1999
Document 3: surviving spouse of a service-disabled veteran who acquires the ownership interest in a small business of the deceased veteran
eligibility for the Department of Veterans Affairs' service-disabled small business contracting goals and preferences

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States related to the temporary suspension of duties on various products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on mixtures of difenoconazole and mefenoxam.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on yarn of carded cashmere yarn coarser than 19.35 metric.

topic.name: Federal Appropriations and Authorizations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the authorization and appropriation of federal funds for various government activities and programs.

Document 1: Amends the Secure Rural Schools and Community Self-Determination Act of 2000 to extend the Act through FY2013.
Requires counties participating in county projects to submit a specified notification for each project for which the participating county obligated county funds.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2011, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Authorizes the head of the Department of Defense (DOD), each of the military departments, the Coast Guard, and NASA to use procedures other than competitive procedures when the contract is for a military construction project.
Determines that it is in its best interest to limit the sources for bids or proposals to prospective contractors with a physical presence in that state.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various aspects of telecommunications services, including consumer protection, billing practices, and universal service funding.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring warning labels on baby monitors
alert users that video and audio from such devices may be intercepted
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit local exchange carriers or providers of interconnected VoIP service from placing a third-party charge
subjects violators to civil forfeiture and specified penalties
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require all telecommunications carriers to contribute to the universal service fund
establishes within the universal service fund the Broadband for Unserved Areas Account

topic.name: U.S. Government Procedures and Elections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative procedures related to statehood, congressional appointments, and election integrity in the United States.

Document 1: Sets forth procedures for admission into the United States of the state of New Columbia.
Requires the Mayor of the District of Columbia to submit to the eligible voters propositions for statehood.
Document 2: Establishes the Daniel Webster Congressional Clerkship Program for the appointment of individuals who are graduates of accredited law schools to serve as Congressional Clerks.
Requires the committees to ensure that Congressional Clerks selected under this Act are apportioned equally between majority and minority party offices.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to make it unlawful for a chief state election administration official to take active part in political management.
States that the budgetary effects of this Act shall be determined by reference to the latest statement titled 'Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation' for this Act.

topic.name: Education Policy for Underserved Communities
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving educational access, quality, and cultural relevance for Native American and low-income students.

Document 1: establish an in-school facility innovation program contest to encourage institutions of higher education to solve the problem of how to improve Indian school facilities
expand programs for Native American school children that support learning in their Native language and culture
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to local educational agencies to promote and strengthen as an integral part of the curriculum one or more of the subjects of music and arts, foreign languages, civics and government
Gives priority to LEAs that have the greatest numbers or percentages of children from families below the poverty line
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states, or local educational agencies in states that do not receive such grants, to reduce the size of core curriculum classes
Requires grant funds to be used for constructing new classroom space, hiring additional teachers

topic.name: Forest Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on forest recovery, conservation, and species protection in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to commence within 30 days after enactment of this Act and complete within 75 days an evaluation of the Wallow Fire Area
to identify projects to reduce such risks by removing hazard trees and fire-damaged, dead, and dying timber resources in such Area
Document 2: Designates the federal land within the Monongahela National Forest in West Virginia, to be known as the North Fork Mountain Wilderness, as a wilderness area
for inclusion in the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS)
Document 3: This bill amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the Department of the Interior or the Department of Commerce, as appropriate, to provide to affected states all data that is used as the basis of a determination on whether a species is an endangered species or a threatened species before making a determination
The appropriate Department must use data submitted by a state, tribal, or county government in making such a determination.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the regulation and oversight of financial institutions and practices, particularly in relation to investor protection and auditing standards.

Document 1: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to extend the regulatory jurisdiction of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
establish standards for independence to be used by a registered public accounting firm
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Labor from prescribing any regulation under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974
Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to prohibit the SEC from promulgating a rule establishing an investment advisor standard of conduct

topic.name: Public Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving welfare and care standards for vulnerable populations in the United States.

Document 1: establishing standards for short-term custody of individuals
consistent with national standards under the Prison Rape Elimination Act
Document 2: provide access to secure, convenient package delivery receptacles
consider the impact of weather conditions, physical barriers, or any other factor on the feasibility of providing a primary mode of mail delivery
Document 3: transfer unclaimed clothing recovered at airport security checkpoints to local veterans organizations
distribution to homeless or needy veterans and their families

topic.name: Food and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of food, drugs, and consumer products to ensure safety, efficacy, and transparency in manufacturing and labeling.

Document 1: Applies this Act to replacement equipment, food, drugs, devices, cosmetics, biological products, consumer products, meat or meat food product, poultry or poultry products, and eggs or egg products
Prohibits a manufacturer from distributing a covered product unless the manufacturer holds a certification from the U.S. Customs and Border Protection
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to permit brand name drug applications to rely upon investigations conducted in certain foreign countries
The FDA must prioritize the review of, and act within 150 days on, generic drug applications or supplements to generic drug applications
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem a drug to be misbranded unless the labeling of such drug lists the identity of the country of manufacture of each active ingredient
listed in descending order based on the percentage of the number of such ingredients in the final dosage form manufactured in such countries

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts related to construction, safety education, and efficiency in government contracting.

Document 1: Monuments Assembled and Domestically Engineered Act or the MADE Act
Requires the Secretary of the Interior or the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to determine compliance with material sourcing requirements.
Document 2: Campus Fire Safety Education Act of 2016
Directs the Department of Education to establish a program to award grants for fire safety education.
Document 3: Design-Build Efficiency and Jobs Act of 2014
Modifies the procedure for awarding design and construction contracts for federal buildings.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support for small businesses through cybersecurity, international trade, and bonding assistance.

Document 1: amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make grants to small business development centers (SBDCs)
methods for the provision of counsel and assistance to improve small businesses' cyber security infrastructure
Document 2: establish an Associate Administrator for International Trade as the head of the Office of International Trade of the Small Business Administration (SBA)
enhance the export capabilities of small businesses and small manufacturers
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation, acting through the Minority Resource Center, to prescribe, guarantee, and enter into commitments to guarantee a surety against loss
requires the Secretary to establish a program under which not more than five selected states may carry out activities of the Secretary with respect to issuing surety guarantees

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer protections in credit and housing finance.

Document 1: Credit Card Accountability Responsibility and Disclosure Act of 2007
Prohibits penalties for on-time payments and fees for non-use of credit card.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements under the HOPE for Homeowners Program
Requires the mortgagor to agree in writing that the mortgagor shall be liable to repay any direct financial benefit.
Document 3: Housing Financial Literacy Act of 2015
Revises the single insurance premium payment for a mortgage for first-time homebuyers.

topic.name: Land Conveyance and Exchange
topic.description: The documents discuss the processes and regulations surrounding the conveyance and exchange of federal and state lands, including specific provisions and requirements for such transactions.

Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal land in Grand, San Juan, and Uintah Counties, Utah, to the state of Utah
Requires the conveyance to be in three phases
